video_type_check.reasoning: Only 1 real conversation with a target. The 'bad approaches' are intentional demonstrations for the camera, not real approaches. ~70% of the video is camera-facing instructional monologue. Closer to talking_head with embedded infield demo than a compilation of multiple approaches.

conversation_verdicts.notes: Segments 54-55 are partly camera-facing instruction while still in the approach. Segment 63 bleeds from conversation closing into commentary ('okay so there' begins commentary). Minor boundary bleed but does not corrupt the conversation.

other_flags: Segment 63 contains within-segment boundary bleed: conversation closing ('have an awesome day') transitions mid-segment into commentary ('okay so there'). Not fixable by segment-level reclassification.
Segment 0 (0.0-0.8s) is a very short fragment ('thought you looked really cute') that may be a transcript artifact or cold-open snippet — too brief to classify meaningfully.

other_flags_detailed.notes: Segment transitions from conv 1 closing to commentary mid-segment. Would need sub-segment split to fix.
0.8s fragment at video start — may be a cold-open snippet or transcript artifact.

summary: Roles and collapse overrides are correct. Main concern is video type: classified as 'compilation' but has only 1 real approach amid extensive camera-facing instruction — closer to talking_head. Minor boundary bleed at segment 63 where conversation closing runs into commentary within one segment.
